senses.definition: a genus of plants in the carrot family, with finely dissected leaves and small white flowers
examples: Ptilimnium species are often found in wetlands.
The garden included a specimen of Ptilimnium.

senses.definition: relating to a part of an insect's head where a ptilinum (a temporary bladder) is located
examples: ptilinal suture is a feature examined in entomology texts.
The ptilinal structure is important in some Diptera.

senses.definition: alternate spelling/variant of ptilinum, a temporary bladderlike organ on the head of some fly pupae used to help emergence
examples: The ptilinum inflates to help the fly emerge from the puparium.
Ptilinum mechanisms are described in classic entomology texts.

senses.definition: a genus of treeshrews, the sole member of the family Ptilocercidae
examples: Ptilocercus lowii is the only living species in its family.
Ptilocercus is considered one of the most primitive treeshrews.

senses.definition: a taxonomic family name in entomology (historical or less common), often associated with a group related to ptilinum-bearing insects
examples: References to Ptilonorhynchidae appear in older taxonomic treatments.
Some classifications have revised relationships within this group.

senses.definition: a subfamily name within a broader insect family, used in taxonomic contexts
examples: Ptilonorhynchinae was described in some early classifications.
The subfamily includes several genera studied by entomologists.

senses.definition: a taxonomic term likely referring to a genus or group within Diptera or related insect orders
examples: Ptilopaedes is cited in entomological literature
Further study of Ptilopaedes is needed to clarify classification.

senses.definition: relating to ptilopaedes or to the ptilinum area in insects; used in technical descriptions
examples: The ptilopaedic region is of interest in Diptera anatomy studies.
A ptilopaedic process is described in the entomology notes.

senses.definition: plural of ptilosis, a term in biology for a small fissure or seam-like structure; or a swooping change in shape in certain organisms
examples: The ptiloses in the specimen were carefully documented.
Ptiloses can indicate developmental stages.

senses.definition: a biological term for a seam-like or fissure-like structure, or a change in form in certain organisms
examples: The ptilosis of the shell indicates growth stages.
Researchers noted a deep ptilosis in the fossil specimen.

senses.definition: a genus of small, smooth-shelled beads or pebbles used as a toy? (Note: in biology, could be a mis-spelling or rare term; commonly associated with paleontology or ichthyology contexts)
examples: In some editions, ptilota refers to a small structural element.
The term appears in old natural history catalogs.

senses.definition: a member of the family Ptinidae, a group of beetles commonly known as spider beetles
examples: Ptinids are often found in stored products.
The pest control report mentioned several ptinids.

senses.definition: the family Ptinidae, a group of beetles including spider beetles and furniture beetles
examples: Ptinidae includes several common household pests.
Taxonomic keys help identify different ptinids.

senses.definition: resembling or relating to the Ptinidae beetles; similar in form or type
examples: ptinoid features resemble those of ptinids.
The specimen shows ptinoid characteristics.

senses.definition: a genus within the Ptinidae family of beetles
examples: Ptinus species are studied in pest biology.
A specimen belonged to the genus Ptinus.

senses.definition: a coarse barley flour used in making a thin, nourishing drink; historically used in brewing or as a medicinal preparation
examples: A cup of ptisan was prescribed for the sick.
Ptisan was common in ancient cookery and medicine.

senses.definition: plural of ptisan; coarse barley flours used to make nourishing drinks
examples: The ptisans prepared by the apothecary were shared among the sick.
Old recipes mention several ptisans.

senses.definition: plural of a term meaning a form of government characterized by weak or incompetent rule (rare or historical usage)
examples: The text discusses several metanic ptochocracies.

senses.definition: a form of government where power is in the hands of incompetent rulers
examples: Some theorists used the term ptochocracy to criticize poor governance.

senses.definition: the origin or birth of a form of government or a body part; used in technical or historical discourse
examples: The chapter discusses ptochogony in ancient political theory.

senses.definition: the study of ptochocracies and related political concepts; a discipline in political theory
examples: Ptochology examined the dynamics of weak regimes.

senses.definition: relating to Ptolemy or the Ptolemaic dynasty; also used to describe things associated with the ancient astronomer-king
examples: A work of Ptolemaean origin discusses celestial mappings.
The style is sometimes called Ptolemaean in antiquarian texts.

senses.definition: relating to Ptolemy or to the era of the Ptolemaic dynasty; used in scholarly contexts
examples: A Ptolemaian map shows ancient world geography.
Ptolemaian astronomy influenced medieval science.

senses.definition: relating to the ancient Greek-Egyptian dynasty founded by Ptolemy; also describing the geocentric system of astronomy
examples: The Ptolemaic model places Earth at the center of the universe.
Ptolemaic law impacted ancient governance in some regions.

senses.definition: relating to the Ptolemaic period or to Ptolemy; historically used to describe things of that era
examples: Ptolemaical diagrams illustrate geocentric ideas.
The manuscript has a ptolemaical style.
